raw_consumer_statement:
Issue Type : Credit card billing dispute not properly resolved Company : Barclays Bank Delaware I am filing this complaint under the Fair Credit Billing Act ( 15 U.S.C. 1666 ). 

I disputed a {$1400.00} charge from XXXX XXXX for failure to deliver the contracted service. Barclays initially issued a provisional credit, then reversed it after accepting the merchants explanation that I cancelled too late. 

This is incorrect. 

XXXX own documentation states they send the rental voucher 48 hours before pickup. Our rental began XX/XX/XXXX. The voucher was not sent until XX/XX/XXXX, after the rental start time had passed. 

The voucher contains the rental supplier name and pickup location. Without it, we did not know where to retrieve the vehicle. It was impossible to perform under the contract. 

This is not a late cancellation issue. This is non-delivery of a prepaid service. 

Under 15 U.S.C. 1666 ( b ) ( 3 ), a billing error includes charges for goods or services not delivered as agreed.

Barclays failed to properly apply this standard and instead accepted the merchants mischaracterization of the dispute.
